reasoning: The question evaluates whether the Chicago Fed NFCI Credit Subindex will be higher on 2026-03-22 compared to its value on 2026-03-15. Based on FRED data up to March 13, 2026, the index has exhibited a very strong and smooth upward momentum (tightening credit conditions) over the last 6 weeks: Jan 30 (-0.036), Feb 6 (-0.033), Feb 13 (-0.027), Feb 20 (-0.020), Feb 27 (-0.011), Mar 6 (-0.003), and Mar 13 (0.005). The weekly increases have stabilized at around +0.008. There is some ambiguity in how the resolution dates map to observation dates (e.g. comparing the most recent available on those dates: March 13 vs. March 6, or comparing March 20 vs. March 13). If it resolves based on comparing the March 13 release to the March 6 release, it has already increased (0.005 > -0.003). If it relies on the upcoming March 20 data, the perfectly uninterrupted upward momentum over the last month strongly implies the trend will continue or remain elevated above the previous week's value. The probability of an abrupt reversal large enough to negate the established +0.008 weekly run-rate is relatively low. Thus, I assign an 84% probability of an increase.
